comment.body:
ES VWAP at 5745 - hard resistance.
Without clearing out 5715, and showing liquidity, we will retest the given lows. But there I expect strong buying liquidity.
Rangebound market, for non-linear derivatives there is better r/r on the short delta side. 
https://stochvoltrader.substack.com/p/after-nfp-fed-weekly-post-05-09may

comment.body:
35% Drop in Cargo Volume: Gene Seroka, Executive Director of the Port of Los Angeles, warns of a significant decline in cargo volume starting the week of May 5, 2025, due to US tariffs on Chinese goods.
Decline in Ship Arrivals: The port has seen a 44% year-over-year decline in scheduled container ship arrivals from China for the week of May 4-10, 2025, with only 12 ships expected compared to 22 the previous year.
Supply Chain Disruption: This supply shock could disrupt US supply chains, potentially leading to broader economic impacts and inflation, similar to post-COVID disruptions.
Impact on Retailers: Major retailers are halting shipments from China, contributing to the decline in cargo volume and potential supply chain issues.
Port of Los Angeles' Significance: As North America's busiest container port, any disruptions to the Port of Los Angeles could have significant economic implications.

comment.body:
✅ **SPX IF/THEN Pathing – Assume Spot = 5640**

---

**IF > 5640**, THEN
→ test 5650
→ IF > 5650, THEN path toward 5675
→ IF > 5675, THEN possible stall at 5700
→ IF > 5700, THEN faster move possible toward 5730

---

**IF < 5640**, THEN
→ test 5635 support
→ IF < 5635, THEN 5625 becomes key pivot
→ IF < 5625, THEN opens drop to 5540/35 support zone
→ IF < 5535, THEN potential magnet to 5500

--- moves may remain reactive and exaggerated.

comment.body: Yep, and I mentioned it a few weeks ago. China workers strikes are going to become more prominent this month.  "Protests by workers demanding unpaid wages are spreading across China as factory closures increase due to high U.S. tariffs and a slowing domestic economy, according to a report by Radio Free Asia (RFA). From Hunan's Dao County to Sichuan’s Suining and Inner Mongolia’s Tongliao, workers have taken to the streets over delayed payments and job losses."
